description: arXiv:2607.18004v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many visual reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms learn representations by matching latent distances to a behavioral distance induced by reward and transition similarity. In practice, the choice of the latent distance can strongly affect performance: using a fixed, pre-specified global norms (e.g., $\ell_p$ norms or other hand-designed metrics) m...
